title: Require clean committed Work Item bundle before PR validation
outOfScope: adopter product runtime behavior outside the PR-boundary lifecycle fixture

sources.reason: PR gate must reject a dirty worktree so generated release evidence is committed into the PR.
Regression coverage for dirty and clean PR boundary.
Adopter lifecycle regression models the required commit before PR validation.
Preflight must distinguish reviewed empty unknowns from omitted risk analysis.
Regression coverage for medium-risk Contracts with explicit empty-unknown review.
Summary validation must not require its own result before it executes.
Regression coverage for the non-self-blocking aiSummary check.
Documents the archive, commit, PR aggregate check, and PR order.
Keeps the primary lifecycle guide aligned with the enforced PR boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: The guard changes the PR entry condition for both template maintenance and adopter lifecycle validation; it does not change adopter product runtime or publication.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized root-cause process correction before publication.

preReviewWarnings: Verify the finalization command is only usable after archive creation and before PR merge; publication remains blocked until post-merge closure evidence is verified.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Dirty release evidence or other uncommitted paths are rejected at PR boundary.
A clean committed PR bundle passes the boundary gate in template and adopter flows.

acceptance: PR aggregate check fails closed when the repository has uncommitted release evidence or any uncommitted path at PR boundary.
A clean committed PR bundle passes the new boundary gate and ordinary paired ownership remains unchanged in template and adopter flows.
Regression coverage proves dirty rejection and clean acceptance in template and adopter flows.
The lifecycle command sequence is enforced as archive, commit the complete bundle, check-ai-pr, push, and PR; CI validates the same committed bundle.
Preflight returns Unknowns=Ready only when riskAssessment.unknownsReviewComplete=true and unknowns=[]; otherwise it returns a blocking non-ready signal.
A fresh Summary with all other required checks passed is accepted by check-ai-change-summary before aiSummary evidence is recorded; the Finish runner records the result afterward.
Archive-growth and code-line budgets are hard-gated in this functional process correction; no budget-only Work Item is created.
guidelines: Use canonical finalizer/preflight commands; do not hand-edit generated release evidence.
Keep release-process changes scoped to the template repository and distinguish template from adopter runtime.
Do not publish tags or provider assets from this Work Item.

intent.problem: The v2 release-freeze Work Item generated final release evidence after archive creation but did not commit those generated files before creating the PR. CI validated the clean remote PR tree, while ai-close later found the local worktree dirty and the merged tree lacked the final freeze evidence.
constraints: The PR boundary must be evaluated on a clean worktree and index.
Do not weaken ordinary paired ownership.
Do not publish in this Work Item.
Do not rewrite immutable archived evidence.
intent.rationale: Make the missing commit boundary fail closed before PR creation, so generated release evidence cannot exist only in the local worktree.

rawUserRequest: 发现 PR/CI 流程问题时停止发布并从根本上改善；预算、格式化和提交边界必须在事前硬检查，不创建单独预算工单。

declaredIntent.summary: Require a clean committed release PR boundary before publication.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized root-cause governance process correction before release.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

repaymentRecords.reason: Functional commit-boundary correction; no budget-only Work Item, repay during the next functional release_identity_stabilization Work Item.
Measured PR boundary guard and regression coverage; no budget-only Work Item, repay during the next functional release_identity_stabilization Work Item.
